role_title: Senior Manager, New Verticals - In Store Intelligence Strategy & Operations

location.office_time_required: majority of team is in New York; spend time working together in-office and remotely

level_signals: 8+ years of experience in high-performance cultures
2+ years of experience managing high-performing teams
Reports into a Director on Strategy & Operations team
Comp range $170K–$250K base consistent with senior manager at DoorDash

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 8+ years of experience. You've succeeded in high-performance cultures (whether in tech, operations, strategy, consulting, banking, or a related experience).
You have 2+ years of experience managing high-performing teams.
you're comfortable pulling your own data in SQL or modeling in Excel.
You have experience owning metrics with complex, multi-input attribution; comfort partnering with ML and engineering on technical tradeoffs
You solve problems from first principles. You're excited to solve problems in innovative ways, and you resist "this is how it's always been done" thinking.
You're unafraid of ambiguity. Rapid change and learning is exciting to you, and you thrive in ambiguous, startup environments
You'll work with a cross-functional team, including product, engineering, ML, design, and operations, and lead your own pod to build this new line of business for DoorDash.

You'll lead high-impact initiatives from concept to execution — defining strategy, building roadmaps, partnering cross-functionally across product, engineering, and ML, and using data to continuously optimize outcomes.
You have completed your BS/BA Degree, ideally a business or engineering focused concentration.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: prior experience in marketplace ops, fulfillment, or data-intensive platform environments a plus.

scope.team_size_estimate: unclear (directly manages a pod; size not stated)
scope.surface: DoorDash ground truth AI platform — in-store data collection and intelligence across tens of thousands of physical store locations; inventory accuracy, Dasher pick time, customer selection, catalog, and pricing
scope.business_model: marketplace / platform (B2B2C — merchant partners, Dashers, consumers); Grocery, Convenience, Alcohol, Retail verticals
core_responsibilities: Own the operational engine behind DoorDash's ground truth AI platform
Build and scale technology that collects in-store data across tens of thousands of physical store locations
Translate in-store data into measurable improvements in inventory accuracy, Dasher pick time, and customer selection
Design systems that capture real-time shelf data and define how that data flows into downstream product experiences
Lead high-impact initiatives from concept to execution — defining strategy, building roadmaps
Partner cross-functionally across product, engineering, and ML
Scale a nascent platform to the majority of DoorDash's non-restaurant GMV

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed ($170K–$250K base) and competitive for a senior manager role
Highly visible role with clear platform ownership (not a vague project)
Novel problem space at intersection of computer vision, in-store logistics, and marketplace health
Comprehensive benefits package described in detail
Role reports directly to a Director — clear organizational placement
red_flags: Role requires New York-based candidate; remote not eligible — restricts geographic pool
Compensation range is wide ($80K spread), suggesting significant level/title ambiguity at time of offer
No explicit visa sponsorship statement — unclear for international candidates
Covey AI screening tool used for NYC roles — candidates subject to AEDT evaluation

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years requirements, people management requirement, comp range, and reporting line)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities and platform description; not enumerated as KPIs in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and verticals listed)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from 'looking for someone based in New York as the majority of our team is here')
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from 'base salary is localized according to an employee's work location')
ambiguities: Visa sponsorship not mentioned anywhere in the JD; treated as 'unclear'
The JD says 'hybrid model' and 'looking for someone based in New York' but does not specify required in-office days per week — office time requirement quoted verbatim rather than quantified
The national base pay range listed ($170K–$250K) is very wide; actual offer likely depends heavily on specific location and experience level
